title: #eng-platform: 1739378821
preview:
Ethan Park: Question for folks thinking about route-level enablement: should the route config allow setting an explicit quant profile ID (ex: `qp_llama3_70b_int8_v3`) OR do we only allow aliases like `recommended` / `latest`?

Mina Haddad: My gut says explicit IDs only. Aliases feel like a footgun unless we define *...
text:
Ethan Park: Question for folks thinking about route-level enablement: should the route config allow setting an explicit quant profile ID (ex: `qp_llama3_70b_int8_v3`) OR do we only allow aliases like `recommended` / `latest`?

Mina Haddad: My gut says explicit IDs only. Aliases feel like a footgun unless we define *exactly* when they resolve. Determinism + audit gets messy if `recommended` changes out from under you.

Ravi Menon: +1. Policy engine needs deterministic behavior. If alias resolves at request time, then two requests with same route config could apply different profiles depending on publish cadence.

Benji Okafor: From API ergonomics POV customers will ask for “just give me the best one”. Aliases are attractive. But we can do it in Console UX without storing it as alias maybe?

Jordan Lee: The determinism requirement is pretty strict. If we support `recommended`, I’d prefe...

text:
Marissa Cole: now that route experiments is GA-ish (at least rollout plan is locked), can we list the post-GA followups in one place? mostly: (1) stat sig / analysis improvements, (2) possible custom metric ingestion roadmap. dropping notes here so we don’t lose them.

Dylan Brooks: +1. also would be good to capture what we *won’t* do (so this doesn’t turn into “console is an analytics product”).

Jada Williams: agreed. right now results API is “basic deltas + guardrails + trend charts”. we should be explicit what analysis is MVP vs future.

Logan Wright: starting list (telemetry/analytics angle):
- significance testing: we currently show deltas without claiming significance. followup: add optional “confidence” signal based on sample size / variance.
- custom metrics ingestion: today only Redwood-defined metrics + eval streams. followup: explore limited customer-defined metrics, but n...

title: #eng-platform: 1739318220
preview:
Benji Okafor: seeing flaky behavior in the promote endpoint under retries. In the e2e test we sometimes “double-promote” from stage 1 -> 2 -> 3 when Console retries after a 502. We need to lock down idempotency + optimistic concurrency before beta.

Benji Okafor: current behavior: `POST /v1/dedicated/pools/{pool_id}...
text:
Benji Okafor: seeing flaky behavior in the promote endpoint under retries. In the e2e test we sometimes “double-promote” from stage 1 -> 2 -> 3 when Console retries after a 502. We need to lock down idempotency + optimistic concurrency before beta.

Benji Okafor: current behavior: `POST /v1/dedicated/pools/{pool_id}/rollout_plans/{plan_id}:promote` is not idempotent. If Console times out but server commits, retry does another promote.

Jordan Lee: +1, we should treat promote like a write action that must be at-most-once per client intent.

Jordan Lee: proposal:
1) require `Idempotency-Key` header for action endpoints (`promote`, `abort`, `hold`, `force_promote`).
2) require an expected version for optimistic concurrency (either `If-Match: W/"plan_version"` or explicit `expected_plan_version` in body).
3) server stores the idempotency record keyed by `(plan_id, action_type, idempotency...

preview:
Ethan Park: Locking the final list of “sensitive admin workflows” + mapping to `event_type` + required fields. This is the list we’re gating Phase 2 coverage on (Hosted/Dedicated/Private parity). Please reply w/ :thumbsup: if your area looks correct, or call out gaps by EOD.

Ethan Park: Canonical required fields (p...
text:
Ethan Park: Locking the final list of “sensitive admin workflows” + mapping to `event_type` + required fields. This is the list we’re gating Phase 2 coverage on (Hosted/Dedicated/Private parity). Please reply w/ :thumbsup: if your area looks correct, or call out gaps by EOD.

Ethan Park: Canonical required fields (per schema v1) for *all* event_types below:
- `schema_version`, `event_id`, `occurred_at`
- `event_type`, `event_category` (admin|security|policy|deployment)
- `tenant_id` (+ `project_id` when scoped)
- `actor_type` (user|service|system), `actor_id`, `actor_display_name` (if user)
- `impersonated_by_actor_id` (nullable)
- `source_ip` (nullable for system), `user_agent` (nullable)
- `request_id` and/or `trace_id` (one required; both preferred)
- `outcome` (success|failure) + `error_code` (if failure)
- `resource_type`, `resource_id` (or `resource_name` if ID not available), `...

title: #eng-platform: 1739146122

text:
Kaitlyn Nguyen: Need a decision on RBAC for route experiments before we lock the GA roles. Specifically: who can Start/Stop an experiment? And do we need a break-glass path for oncall?

Marissa Cole: +1. Beta customers asked “who in my org can stop an experiment if it’s blowing up prod.” We need a simple story in Console.

Sean Gallagher: For oncall: we *must* be able to stop quickly even if the customer team misconfigured RBAC / no one’s online. But also we can’t turn “stop” into “edit routing policy.”

Marcus Lin: Security perspective: “stop experiment” is a production-impacting action. Should be least-privileged but not blocked by needing full route admin.

Dylan Brooks: Current proposal in Console backend is `experiments:write` for create/update/start/stop. That’s too coarse.

Chloe Martin: Yeah right now it’s a single gate on mutation endpoints. We can split actions:
- create/upd...

```

title: #eng-platform: 1739029155
preview:
Jordan Lee: Trying to land a decision on gate eval window lengths for Dedicated staged rollouts. Current draft says 5m windows for latency/error/sat. SRE feedback was “too twitchy” esp. bursty pools. Thoughts on 5m vs 15m? Also: do we require min sample size before allowing promote?

Peter Holtz: +1 on twitchy. 5m i...
text:
Jordan Lee: Trying to land a decision on gate eval window lengths for Dedicated staged rollouts. Current draft says 5m windows for latency/error/sat. SRE feedback was “too twitchy” esp. bursty pools. Thoughts on 5m vs 15m? Also: do we require min sample size before allowing promote?

Peter Holtz: +1 on twitchy. 5m is fine if you have steady traffic, but Dedicated pools can be quiet then spike. You’ll oscillate between PASS/FAIL on noise.

Paula Mendes: product angle: operators want “fast feedback” at 1% stage, but we can’t have gates flapping every click. Could do 5m *after* enough samples, else hold as INSUFFICIENT_DATA?

Logan Wright: from telemetry side: 5m is also more sensitive to ingestion lag. We’ve seen 2-4m delays in worst case (pipeline backpressure). If you query “last 5m” you can be effectively looking at 1m of data.

Rafael Mendes: SRE preference: 15m for automated gates,...

If `stage_id` is a UUID per rollout plan, we’ll create a new time series per stage per rollout per pool per region *forever*.

text:
Logan Wright: Heads up on staged rollouts work — adding `stage_id` as a metrics label is going to blow up cardinality. :warning: We’re about to merge PR-28521 (stage tags in metrics/traces). If `stage_id` is a UUID per rollout plan, we’ll create a new time series per stage per rollout per pool per region *forever*.

Caleb Johnson: +1. I pulled a quick estimate from current Dedicated label sets. Right now we already have `pool_id` + `model` + `route` + `region` and some request dimensions. If we add `stage_id` (high entropy) it’s basically unbounded.

Allison Grant: Yup, this hits the observability standards doc: labels must be low-cardinality + bounded. `stage_id` is neither. We can do stage attribution but we need to be deliberate: allowlist + bounded representation.

Mina Haddad: Routing side: stage_id is in the control plane model as the stable identifier for the stage object. Cons...

text:
Marissa Cole: hey all — need to finalize GA defaults for route experiments this week. Specifically: required fields (what Console enforces vs warns), max variants, stickiness key precedence, and UI guardrails. Can we converge in this thread so Connor can lock release notes + defaults?

Dylan Brooks: +1. We keep getting questions from beta customers “what happens if I don’t set sticky key?” and “why did my split skew”. would love GA to be opinionated.

Jada Williams: re: backend: we can enforce a couple things at create/start time without risk. Others should be warnings to avoid breaking existing configs on upgrade.

Jordan Lee: current evaluator precedence (from PR-28433 + follow-ups):
1) explicit sticky key from request context (header / SDK-provided)
2) `customer_id` (tenant)
3) `end_user_id` (if provided)
4) `session_id` (if provided)
5) fallback: request_id (NOT stable; only to av...

text:
Ethan Park: Trying to lock rollout sequencing for audit logging v1 so we don’t thrash across teams. Proposal: (1) schema lib + validator, (2) control plane producers (RBAC + API keys), (3) console backend + UI workflows, (4) private installer parity. Thoughts / gotchas?

Priya Natarajan: +1 on sequencing. The dependency graph is real: everyone needs the schema + event_type registry first or we’ll ship 4 shapes. Let’s make it explicit as a gate for downstream PRs.

Connor O'Brien: For release coordination: can we define “Phase gates” w/ checkboxes? I need something I can paste into the release train notes.

Ethan Park: Yep. Gate 0: merged schema library + JSON Schema artifact + validator. Gate 1: control plane emitting v1 events for RBAC + API key lifecycle behind feature flag (per-tenant). Gate 2: console emits/validates and UI flows audited. Gate 3: private installer emits install/up...

Connor O'Brien: Kicking off coordination thread for tier→pool mapping rollout (feature-flagged). Goal: ship safely w/ guardrails + canary plan. Current PR is `pr-19366-routing-tier-to-pool-selector` behind `routing.tier_pool_mapping`. Need alignment on: default behavior, fallback rules, canary accounts, and what dashboards/alerts we watch.

Mina Haddad: thx. top concern: we can’t route Premium traffic to a pool that doesn’t have headroom and accidentally create a self-inflicted SLO miss. also need deterministic behavior when mapping is missing / stale.

Ravi Menon: +1. I want to avoid “surprise” routing changes for existing Dedicated deployments. Would prefer:
- Phase 0: compute mapping + emit decision logs/metrics (shadow)
- Phase 1: enforce for allowlisted deployments only
- Phase 2: roll out tier-by-tier

Talia Benson: trying to lock down migration + backfill plan for rollout_plans schema. release train wants a slot. can we align on sequencing today?

Connor O'Brien: yep. we have a DB change window on Thu 10:00–12:00 PT for control plane. anything that needs long-running backfill needs to be *outside* the locky migration step.

Benji Okafor: current PR has new tables + columns for `dedicated_pool_rollout_plans` + `stages` + audit hooks. main question: backfill existing dedicated pools w/ a default plan row or leave nil until first use.

Ethan Park: for v1, i’d rather not create plans for every pool automatically in the migration itself. too many pools + risk of timeouts. can we do lazy-create on first write + optional async backfill for analytics?

Priya Natarajan: +1 to lazy-create. migration should be additive + fast. backfill as a job behind a flag.
```

title: #eng-platform: 1738624112
preview:
Ethan Park: trying to lock down rollout plan state machine today so we can finish API shape + migrations. current proposal: `DRAFT -> RUNNING -> COMPLETED` and `RUNNING <-> HELD`, `RUNNING|HELD -> ABORTED`. terminal = COMPLETED/ABORTED. anything missing?

Benji Okafor: +1. question: do we need an intermediate `ABORT...
text:
Ethan Park: trying to lock down rollout plan state machine today so we can finish API shape + migrations. current proposal: `DRAFT -> RUNNING -> COMPLETED` and `RUNNING <-> HELD`, `RUNNING|HELD -> ABORTED`. terminal = COMPLETED/ABORTED. anything missing?

Benji Okafor: +1. question: do we need an intermediate `ABORTING` or `PROMOTING` state for UI? or just model actions as events and keep state simple.

Jordan Lee: strongly prefer simple state, and represent “in progress” via action_id / audit event status. promote/abort must be idempotent anyway so having `PROMOTING` can create weird stuck states when clients retry.

Chloe Martin: from console perspective we do need to show “promotion in progress” sometimes (user clicks promote, spinner, etc). but that can be client-side / action endpoint returns immediately. if we need server-side progress, we can expose `last_action` fields without...

title: #eng-platform: 1738621407
preview:
Ethan Park: we need to settle API resource shape for Dedicated staged rollouts. question: is rollout plan its own resource, or embedded under dedicated pool config?

Benji Okafor: +1. current draft in my branch is a top-level `DedicatedPoolRolloutPlan` with `pool_id` FK. endpoints like `/v1/dedicated-pool-rollout-pl...
text:
Ethan Park: we need to settle API resource shape for Dedicated staged rollouts. question: is rollout plan its own resource, or embedded under dedicated pool config?

Benji Okafor: +1. current draft in my branch is a top-level `DedicatedPoolRolloutPlan` with `pool_id` FK. endpoints like `/v1/dedicated-pool-rollout-plans/{plan_id}`. but I keep getting the "why not just stick it in pool config" question.

Marissa Cole: from Console POV, I’d *really* prefer it as its own resource. we need audit history + stage timeline + promotion actions without making pool update a giant god-object.

Jordan Lee: also determinism/auditability: promotion/abort are state machine transitions. treating as "update pool config" feels wrong (and makes idempotency messy).

Mina Haddad: routing layer cares about a stable pointer: pool -> active rollout plan -> current stage. if it’s embedded in pool config, every...

preview:
Mei Lin: Heads up: drilldowns on the internal Unit Economics dashboard are still spiky (p95 8–15s) when Finance runs 30d + group_by=model,region,tier. We need a partitioning/materialization plan before we scale dogfood. Can folks weigh in on the warehouse side?

Felix Schneider: +1. The API endpoint `GET /internal/u...
text:
Mei Lin: Heads up: drilldowns on the internal Unit Economics dashboard are still spiky (p95 8–15s) when Finance runs 30d + group_by=model,region,tier. We need a partitioning/materialization plan before we scale dogfood. Can folks weigh in on the warehouse side?

Felix Schneider: +1. The API endpoint `GET /internal/unit-econ/timeseries?group_by=model,region,tier&window=30d` is the main offender. Console is basically doing top-N + timeseries in the same page load. If the underlying view is scanning too much, we’re toast.

Allison Grant: What’s the current layout? Are we still querying the "raw" metering rollup table (the one that has request_id-level rows) or are we on the hourly agg already?

Mei Lin: We’re on hourly agg, but it’s a wide table and not partitioned in a useful way. Current objects:
- `ANALYTICS.UNIT_ECONOMICS_HOURLY_FACT` (hourly aggregated, still ~1.2B rows because of e...

text:
Logan Wright: Question for the group re experiment metrics dims: what are we using as `route_id`? Right now I’m seeing a mix of raw path (`/v1/accounts/123/chat`) and templated (`/v1/accounts/{account_id}/chat`) depending on where the span is created.

Caleb Johnson: +1. In OTel we currently tag `http.route` when we have it, but gateway sometimes only has `http.target` (raw) unless the route matcher passes template down.

Allison Grant: Raw path is going to explode cardinality. We should not be emitting per-request metrics with raw path as a label, especially for anything under customers’ dynamic prefixes.

Jada Williams: Agree. For Console “Route Experiments” we already have a concept of “route” (policy route). That should map to a stable identifier, not raw URL.

Jordan Lee: From policy engine POV, we evaluate experiments after route match. We can attach a stable `route_uuid` (from ...

title: #eng-platform: 1738439933
preview:
Ethan Park: Sanity check before we lock ENG-4830 guidance: for audit events we’re standardizing on `request_id`, `trace_id`, and `admin_action_id` as the correlation fields. Can folks confirm definitions + propagation rules esp across async jobs?

Logan Wright: +1. Telemetry side needs stable semantics. Biggest ask:...
text:
Ethan Park: Sanity check before we lock ENG-4830 guidance: for audit events we’re standardizing on `request_id`, `trace_id`, and `admin_action_id` as the correlation fields. Can folks confirm definitions + propagation rules esp across async jobs?

Logan Wright: +1. Telemetry side needs stable semantics. Biggest ask: `request_id` is per inbound HTTP request, `trace_id` is distributed tracing id (W3C), `admin_action_id` is “user intent” that can span multiple requests / async steps.

Nadia Rahman: Agree. Also: for SIEM + exporter normalization we need them *always present* when available, but not fabricated in a way that breaks investigations.

Caleb Johnson: Definitions I have:
- `trace_id`: OpenTelemetry trace id (hex). Comes from `traceparent` if present, otherwise created by our tracer.
- `request_id`: our gateway/service request identifier. If inbound header `X-Request-Id` exists w...

Alicia Perez: yeah that was in an early v0 wireframe (mostly to ...
text:
Chloe Martin: heads up on a potential footgun in the unit econ dashboard top-N tables. current wireframes show “Top customers by $/1M output tokens” as an option in the breakdown menu. can we sanity check privacy / policy here before we ship anything?

Alicia Perez: yeah that was in an early v0 wireframe (mostly to match what Finance asked for). we can drop it, but wanted to ask eng first if we’re allowed to show customer names internally.

Marissa Cole: +1, we should be careful. even internal, customer-identifiable + unit cost feels like it can leak quickly via screenshots. what’s the rule of thumb from Security?

Felix Schneider: from API contract sketch, we currently accept `group_by=customer_id` but UI doesn’t surface it yet. still, someone could call the endpoint directly.

Nora Ahmed: and top-N tables in the UI are copy/pasteable. we should assume it’ll end up in decks.

Marcus ...

title: #eng-platform: 1738352207
preview:
Sahana Iyer: We need to land build-time enforcement for audit events soon. Question: where do we hook it in CI so it actually catches missing fields / unregistered `event_type`, but doesn’t make every dev hate us? :grimacing:

Ethan Park: +1. Control plane has a bunch of admin endpoints; if this lint is slow or flak...
text:
Sahana Iyer: We need to land build-time enforcement for audit events soon. Question: where do we hook it in CI so it actually catches missing fields / unregistered `event_type`, but doesn’t make every dev hate us? :grimacing:

Ethan Park: +1. Control plane has a bunch of admin endpoints; if this lint is slow or flaky we’ll be bypassing it immediately.

Benji Okafor: Also need an exceptions mechanism. We have some legacy paths that can’t emit the full v1 payload until we refactor auth context.

Allison Grant: Requirements from observability side:
- should run in PR checks, not just nightly
- should have a local runner (`make lint-audit`) that devs can run fast
- output needs to point to exact file/line and the missing required fields

Kaitlyn Nguyen: Another constraint: some producers are in the auth middleware (gateway) and some are in async jobs. The lint can’t assume a single callsi...

title: #eng-platform: 1738200021
preview:
Chloe Martin: we need to settle caching for the unit econ query APIs. perf tests w/ the drilldowns are still spiky, and if Finance is gonna hit QTD views every morning we’re gonna melt the warehouse.

Felix Schneider: +1. for the `GET /internal/unit-econ/timeseries` endpoint we can add a read-through cache keyed on ...
text:
Chloe Martin: we need to settle caching for the unit econ query APIs. perf tests w/ the drilldowns are still spiky, and if Finance is gonna hit QTD views every morning we’re gonna melt the warehouse.

Felix Schneider: +1. for the `GET /internal/unit-econ/timeseries` endpoint we can add a read-through cache keyed on (time_range, filters, group_by). question is TTL. I was thinking 5-10m default.

Mei Lin: FYI the heaviest query is the top-N breakdown by model+region+tier when the user has “include model version” toggled. even w/ the new MV, it’s still ~3-6s cold, and can go 12s when the warehouse is under load.

Logan Wright: Finance wants “fresh” meaning: "if it's 10am, I want to see 9am". but our hourly job doesn’t close until ~15 past the hour on a good day, and we have occasional late-arriving token rows (streaming path).

Rishi Malhotra: jumping in — from FP&A side: for day-to-day ...

title: #eng-platform: 1738186033
preview:
Jada Williams: Need to lock default guardrail thresholds for route experiments (latency/error/cost) before we freeze Console copy. What are we comfortable shipping as defaults for beta + GA?

Paula Mendes: +1. We should pick defaults that are safe (catch real regressions) but not so tight they trigger constantly, es...
text:
Jada Williams: Need to lock default guardrail thresholds for route experiments (latency/error/cost) before we freeze Console copy. What are we comfortable shipping as defaults for beta + GA?

Paula Mendes: +1. We should pick defaults that are safe (catch real regressions) but not so tight they trigger constantly, esp on low traffic routes.

Sean Gallagher: From SRE POV, defaults must be conservative + predictable. Also we need to be explicit: is this soft-stop only by default? (banner + notify), and hard-stop is opt-in / behind kill switch.

Jada Williams: Yes. Per the earlier thread, default behavior = soft-stop + notification. Hard-stop available but opt-in.

Allison Grant: For latency, I’d suggest p99 + p95. p50 is too noisy. Also recommend comparing variant vs control within same window (not historical baseline) so we don’t conflate daily traffic mix changes.

Logan Wright: Teleme...

text:
Logan Wright: need to settle export flow for usage insights before we finalize API contract. async job vs sync download? and hard limits.
Chloe Martin: +1. console wants “Export CSV” button. UX spec currently assumes async (toast + background job) but FE would love a simple sync `GET` if feasible.
Dylan Brooks: from console perspective: sync is nicer for small ranges, but anything over a couple seconds will feel broken. also we need “your export is ready” state.
Nadia Rahman: re: data size: route+model breakdown can blow up if customers filter poorly (30d + all projects + all routes). we should assume some will do worst-case.
Felix Schneider: what’s our current p95 for the breakdown query at 7d? (interactive)
Logan Wright: warehouse says ~900ms-1.5s for typical 24h/7d, but 30d can go 10-30s depending on cardinality + partitions. exports can be bigger + more columns.
Allison Grant: als...
